plugin_desc: Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch-specific SNMP collection (EdgeSwitch-SWITCHING-MIB + EdgeSwitch-BOXSERVICES-PRIVATE-MIB, IANA PEN 4413, Broadcom FASTPATH). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects EdgeSwitch private OIDs for device health: system memory (available/free, KB), chassis temperature (boxServicesTempSensorTemperature) and fan state (boxServicesFanItemState). Fan raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. Memory utilization is computed from (total-free)/total. CPU is exposed only as a per-process table (agentSwitchCpuProcessTable) with no aggregate device-CPU scalar, which telegraf inputs.snmp cannot reduce, so CPU is not collected (N/A); the BOXSERVICES MIB has no power-supply state object, so PSU is also N/A. Applies to EdgeSwitch/UniFi-Switch (the wired switch line); airOS/UniFi wireless APs are a different object and out of scope. Supported brands/models — Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch (ES series).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch system memory (EdgeSwitch-SWITCHING-MIB agentSwitchCpuProcessMemAvailable, reported in KB and scaled to bytes). Together with the free bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_free
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of free bytes in the Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch system memory (EdgeSwitch-SWITCHING-MIB agentSwitchCpuProcessMemFree, reported in KB and scaled to bytes). Low free memory may indicate memory pressure from oversized tables or leaks.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch device, computed as (total - free) / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch device (EdgeSwitch-BOXSERVICES-PRIVATE-MIB boxServicesTempSensorTemperature, a dedicated typed temperature column), aggregated as the maximum across sensors. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan state of the Ubiquiti EdgeSwitch device (EdgeSwitch-BOXSERVICES-PRIVATE-MIB boxServicesFanItemState), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy (operational / powering / not-present) and 2 = fault (failed / no-power / not-powering / incompatible). Aggregated as the maximum across fans so any faulty fan surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a fan failure that can lead to overheating.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
